key_points: Mean summarizes an entire dataset but can be affected by extreme values
Median is useful for datasets containing outliers
Dot plots help identify distribution patterns and clusters in data
Line graphs reveal trends and changes in continuous data over time
Data visualization helps interpret large datasets efficiently
examples: Dot plot showing number of books read by students in a class
Line graph showing population growth over several years
formulas: Mean = Total sum of observations ÷ Number of observations
Median = Middle observation after arranging data in order
Median (even number of observations) = (n/2 th value + (n/2 + 1) th value) / 2
exam_tips: Check whether dataset has odd or even number of values before finding median
Verify calculations when finding mean to avoid arithmetic errors
Ensure scales are equal when plotting line graphs
Interpret graphs carefully before answering questions
